title: Contribution-Format Income Statement
event: Reyes Co. sold 8,000 units at $30. Variable costs are $12 per unit to produce and $3 per unit to sell. Fixed overhead is $60,000 and fixed selling and administrative costs are $20,000. Compute net income under variable costing.

computationPaths.narration: The contribution format sorts by BEHAVIOR (variable vs fixed), not by function — that's what makes it useful for CVP decisions.

memorize.body: Sales − ALL variable costs (production + selling) = CM; CM − ALL fixed costs = income.
Variable SELLING costs belong in the variable block even though they're not product costs — behavior, not function, sorts the statement.

questions.prompt: Using the facts above, variable-costing net income = ?

distractors.feedback: You skipped the variable selling costs (8,000 × $3). ALL variable costs come out before CM.
You expensed only the fixed overhead. Fixed S&A (20,000) is also a period cost: income = 40,000.
